name: Granny's Pumpkin Roll
instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) and position the rack in the center. Grease and flour a 9x13-inch jelly roll pan, or line it with parchment paper for easier removal. 

2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon (note: use cinnamon as pumpkin pie spice substitute) until well combined. 

3. Add the pumpkin puree, eggs, and lemon juice to the dry ingredients. Stir with a wooden spoon or whisk until the batter is smooth and well combined with no lumps. 

4. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and spread it evenly to all corners using a spatula. The layer should be thin and uniform. 

5. Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, until the cake springs back when lightly touched and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Do not overbake or the cake will crack when rolled. 

6. While the cake bakes, lay a clean, damp linen kitchen towel on your counter and generously sprinkle it with powdered sugar to prevent sticking. 

7. As soon as the cake comes out of the oven, immediately turn it out onto the prepared towel. If you used parchment paper, carefully peel it off the cake. 

8. Starting from one short end, carefully roll the warm cake up in the towel lengthwise, creating a spiral. The towel will be rolled inside with the cake. Place seam-side down on a wire rack. 

9. Let the rolled cake cool completely to room temperature, about 1-2 hours. This step is crucial - the cake must be completely cool before unrolling. 

10. Meanwhile, prepare the cream cheese filling: In a medium bowl, beat the cream cheese and butter together with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth and creamy, about 2 minutes. Add the vanilla extract and powdered sugar, beating until light and fluffy, about 2-3 minutes more. 

11. Cover the filling and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up slightly, making it easier to spread. 

12. Once the cake is completely cool, carefully unroll it (it will want to stay curved - that's okay). Spread the chilled cream cheese filling evenly over the entire surface of the cake, leaving a 1/2-inch border around the edges. 

13. Gently re-roll the cake without the towel this time, starting from the same short end. Roll it tightly but carefully to avoid cracking. Wrap the rolled cake tightly in plastic wrap. 

14. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight before slicing. This allows the filling to set and makes slicing much easier. When ready to serve, unwrap, dust with additional powdered sugar if desired, and slice into 1-inch thick rounds.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F and prepare a jelly roll pan. 

2. Mix flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon. 

3. Add pumpkin, eggs, and lemon juice; mix until smooth. 

4. Spread batter evenly in pan. 

5. Bake 12-15 minutes until cake springs back. 

6. Sprinkle powdered sugar on a damp towel. 

7. Turn hot cake onto towel and remove parchment. 

8. Roll cake in towel and cool completely, 1-2 hours. 

9. Beat cream cheese, butter, vanilla, and powdered sugar until fluffy. 

10. Chill filling 30 minutes. 

11. Unroll cooled cake and spread with filling. 

12. Re-roll cake without towel and wrap in plastic. 

13. Refrigerate 2 hours before slicing.
